arabic: يَحْتَفِلُ المُسْلِمُونَ بِعِيدِ الفِطْرِ بَعْدَ انْتِهَاءِ شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ. يَسْتَيْقِظُ النَّاسُ بَاكِرًا لِأَدَاءِ صَلَاةِ العِيدِ فِي المَسْجِدِ. يَرْتَدِي الأَطْفَالُ مَلَابِسَ جَدِيدَةً وَيَحْصُلُونَ عَلَى العَيْدِيَّةِ مِنَ الكِبَارِ. تَصْنَعُ النِّسَاءُ حَلْوَيَاتٍ خَاصَّةً مِثْلَ المَعْمُولِ وَالكَعْكِ. يَتَبَادَلُ الأَقَارِبُ الزِّيَارَاتِ وَالتَّهَانِيَ فِي جَوٍّ مِنَ الفَرَحِ.
translation: Muslimani slave Ramazanski bajram nakon završetka mjeseca ramazana. Ljudi se bude rano kako bi klanjali bajram-namaz u džamiji. Djeca nose novu odjeću i dobijaju bajramluk od odraslih. Žene prave posebne slatkiše poput maamula i kolača. Rođaci razmjenjuju posjete i čestitke u atmosferi radosti.
translationEn: Muslims celebrate Eid al-Fitr after the end of the month of Ramadan. People wake up early to perform the Eid prayer in the mosque. Children wear new clothes and receive 'Eidiya' (money gifts) from adults. Women make special sweets such as maamoul and cookies. Relatives exchange visits and congratulations in an atmosphere of joy.
